title: hieracium sparsum subsp. peristeriense
intro: Hieracium sparsum subsp. peristeriense is a rare, endangered subspecies of the Hawkweed plant, native to Greece, characterized by its small white flowers and narrow leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds and inflammation due to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. The health benefits of Hieracium sparsum subsp. peristeriense include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing, reducing pain and swelling. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve, and is used topically to apply to affected areas.
